search_queries_used: site:gov.ae medication review reconciliation guideline UAE
site:nice.org.uk/guidance/ng5 medicines optimisation medication review reconciliation
site:nice.org.uk/guidance/ng5 structured medication review adherence monitoring concerns
rejection_reasons: Patient pages are not clinician guidance.
Formularies and dose tools were outside this documentation workflow.
Condition-specific algorithms do not support a generic medication-review template.
population_applicability: Broadly applicable to people taking one or more medicines, with structured-review, transfer, reconciliation, age, and polypharmacy qualifiers retained.
setting_applicability: Partial: exact for selected medication-review and reconciliation documentation, not diagnosis, prescribing, dose changes, treatment, referral, or fixed follow-up.
UAE_applicability: No exact current UAE generic medication-review guideline was identified. NICE organisational and service pathways require UAE clinician review.
recency_verification: The current official NICE NG5 recommendations and surveillance status were reviewed on 2026-07-13.
superseded_check: NICE NG5 remains current; its 2019 surveillance found no new evidence affecting the recommendations.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports accurate medicine details, monitoring, and communication at care transitions; no broad legacy field is mapped solely to this section.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports complete and accurate medicine information, prescribed and non-prescribed medicines, allergies, timing, monitoring, and discussion at care transitions.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped medicine-list, prior-prescription, reconciliation, discrepancy, and record fields in qualified reconciliation contexts.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Defines reconciliation as identifying an accurate current medicine list, comparing records, recognising discrepancies, documenting changes, and including over-the-counter or complementary medicines.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped medicine-list, adherence, side-effect, monitoring, concern, discussion, advice-seeking, and structured-review documentation without medicine changes or doses.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports structured review of all medicines, views, understanding, concerns, questions, adherence context, adverse-reaction or side-effect context, monitoring, and circumstances for seeking professional advice.

unresolved_source_gaps: General vitals, abnormal-result red flags, previous-prescription wording outside transfer or reconciliation, generic referral, generic follow-up, fixed intervals, broad return-precaution wording, and the combined history draft remain partly or wholly unsupported.
The workflow does not encode a clear review purpose, polypharmacy, care transition, age, chronic condition, adverse-reaction, or monitoring qualifier.
Medicine selection, starting, stopping, changing, dose, treatment, referral, and exact follow-up timing remain clinician responsibilities.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
